seo.title: Fine-Tuning & Custom Models implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping Fine-Tuning & Custom Models projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist provides a technical roadmap for moving fine-tuned models from experimental notebooks to production-ready inference environments, focusing on data integrity, training efficiency, and deployment stability.

items.title: Schema Validation
items.description: Verify that every training example strictly follows the target model's prompt format (e.g., ChatML, Alpaca, or Llama-3-Instruct) to prevent formatting-induced performance degradation.

items.title: Deduplication and Cleaning
items.description: Run a fuzzy matching script to remove near-duplicate entries in the training set that can cause overfitting on specific phrases.

items.title: Token Length Audit
items.description: Calculate the token count for every example using the target tokenizer to ensure no samples exceed the model's maximum context length during training.

items.title: PII Scrubbing
items.description: Execute a regex or NLP-based scanner to identify and remove Personally Identifiable Information (PII) from the training corpus to meet compliance standards.

items.title: Distribution Balancing
items.description: Verify that the dataset contains a representative ratio of edge cases and common queries to prevent the model from becoming biased toward high-frequency patterns.

items.title: LoRA Rank and Alpha Selection
items.description: Document the choice of LoRA rank (r) and alpha; ensure alpha is typically set to 2x the rank to maintain scaling stability.

items.title: Gradient Accumulation Tuning
items.description: Set gradient accumulation steps to achieve an effective batch size that balances training stability with available VRAM limits.

items.title: Checkpointing Strategy
items.description: Configure the training pipeline to save checkpoints based on validation loss rather than step count to capture the best performing weights.

items.title: Quantization Verification (QLoRA)
items.description: If using QLoRA, verify that the 4-bit NormalFloat (NF4) data type is utilized to maximize memory efficiency without significant precision loss.

items.title: Loss Curve Monitoring
items.description: Integrate Weights & Biases or MLflow to track training vs. validation loss in real-time to detect early-stage divergence or overfitting.

items.title: Hold-out Set Performance
items.description: Test the final adapter against a 10-20% hold-out set that the model never saw during training or hyperparameter tuning.

items.title: Base Model Regression Testing
items.description: Run the fine-tuned model against a set of general-purpose prompts to ensure that domain-specific tuning hasn't caused 'catastrophic forgetting' of basic reasoning.

items.title: Deterministic Output Check
items.description: Run the same prompt through the model multiple times at temperature 0 to ensure output consistency for structured data tasks.

items.title: Human-in-the-Loop Review
items.description: Conduct a blind A/B test where domain experts rank outputs from the fine-tuned model against the base model or previous versions.

items.title: Latency Benchmarking
items.description: Measure Time To First Token (TTFT) and tokens per second (TPS) across different batch sizes to define production hardware requirements.

items.title: Adapter Merging
items.description: Decide between merging LoRA weights into the base model for faster inference or keeping them separate for multi-tenant adapter swapping.

items.title: Inference Engine Optimization
items.description: Validate the model's compatibility with optimized engines like vLLM, TGI, or TensorRT-LLM to maximize throughput.

items.title: Quantization for Production
items.description: Evaluate the performance impact of converting the final model to AWQ or GGUF formats to reduce VRAM footprint in production.

items.title: Health Check Implementation
items.description: Configure a liveness and readiness probe that verifies the model is loaded in VRAM and responding to a simple 'ping' prompt.

items.title: Cold Start Mitigation
items.description: Implement a warm-up strategy for new pods to prevent initial request timeouts while weights are being loaded from storage to GPU memory.

items.title: Model Lineage Tracking
items.description: Store a manifest file with the model binary linking it to the specific dataset version, commit hash of training code, and hyperparameter log.

items.title: Inference Cost Tracking
items.description: Set up monitoring for token usage per API key or user to calculate the unit economics of the custom model deployment.

items.title: Drift Detection Pipeline
items.description: Establish a process to collect production logs for periodic comparison against the training distribution to identify when retraining is necessary.

items.title: License Compliance Audit
items.description: Verify that the base model's license (e.g., Llama 3 Community License) allows for the intended commercial use and distribution of the fine-tuned weights.

items.title: Fallback Logic
items.description: Implement an automated fallback to a larger foundation model or a previous stable version if the fine-tuned model returns an error or low-confidence score.
